items.title: Upgrading From Java 17 To 21: All You Need To Know
items.summary: Java 21 is chock-full of great features but that's for naught of you can't actually upgrade, so I've collected all potential upgrade hurdles and we'll go over every issue that you may encounter on the road from Java 17 to 21

items.summary: See surprising transitions that happens on types when you interoperate between Kotlin and Java.

items.title: Java 21 is no LTS Version - Inside Java Newscast #52
items.summary: Let's separate Java from JDK, OpenJDK from its vendors, and maintenance from support, so we better understand how the ecosystem functions and what long-term support really means.

items.title: Java 21 - The Other Side - Inside Java Newscast #51
items.summary: OMG, how is there even more in JDK 21?! Scoped values preview, key encapsulation mechanism API, a new JFR command, and various API improvements. Generational Shenandoah is out, though, and it doesn't look good for the 32-bit Windows port either.

items.title: Lindy effect in surnames problem
items.summary: Using programming to simulate a problem of the number of surnames in population and observing Lindy effect.

items.title: The beauty of Kotlin type system
items.summary: How type system makes Kotlin so flexible, safe, and intuitive.

items.title: All That is in Java 21?! 😱 - Inside Java Newscast #50
items.summary: JDK 21 is almost too good to be true: It finalizes virtual threads, sequenced collections, generational ZGC, and the pattern matching basics; and evolves and introduces over half a dozen other features.

items.title: Understanding Flow
items.summary: We will explore how flow and its processing really works.

items.title: Script Java Easily in 21 and Beyond - Inside Java Newscast #49
items.summary: To give Java and programming beginners a better learning path, JEP 445 proposes to allow stand-alone main methods that are non-public, non-static, and don't have an args array and we're also JEP draft for multi-file programs

items.title: Scaling Simply with Virtual Threads
items.summary: Virtual threads combine the simplicity of blocking code with the resource efficiency and scalability of reactive programming and in this talk you're going to learn how they do that and how you can use them in your project

items.summary: Learn about the new release of the famous book about Kotlin Coroutines.

items.title: Save 10-20% Memory With Compact Headers - Inside Java Newscast #48
items.summary: JDK Enhancement Proposal 450 proposes to merge a compressed class word into the mark word to reduce object header size on 64-but systems from 96-128 bits to 64 bits, thus saving 10-20% of heap space

items.title: Why you should consider migrating your Gradle scripts to Kotlin DSL
items.summary: Discover the benefits of migrating your Gradle scripts to Kotlin DSL and streamline your development process.

items.title: Getting Rid Of Unused Variables (Finally) - Inside Java Newscast #46
items.summary: JEP 443 proposes to add unnamed variables and patterns to Java. With them, unused variables and patterns can be replaced by a single underscore, which helps writing readable and maintainable code.

items.title: New (Sequenced) Collections In Java 21 - Inside Java Newscast #45
items.summary: All lists, some sets, and some maps have an encounter order, but the collections framework has no type to capture this property and define operations like getting or removing first and last elements or iterating in reverse order. Sequenced collections will fix that in Java 21.

items.title: Interface Delegation

items.title: The Holy Grail of Java Performance - Inside Java Newscast #43
items.summary: The goal of Project Leyden is to improve the startup time, time to peak performance, and footprint of Java programs. Project lead Mark Reinhold recently proposed to extend the Java programming model with features for selectively shifting and constraining computation with condensors. Let's look at his white paper and roadmap.
